title: Chapter 7, Verse 10
shlok:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् ।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥ १०॥
transliteration:
bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam
buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham
translation: O Partha, know Me to be the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent, and the splendor of the splendid.
interpretation: Krishna reveals himself as the bija, the primordial seed from which all existence grows. Just as every plant carries its nature within its seed, every being carries the divine within itself. Buddhi refers to discerning intelligence, not mere cleverness, but the capacity to perceive what is real and act rightly. Tejas means radiance or vital brilliance, the luminosity that makes a person compelling and effective. Krishna is not separate from these qualities but is the very source from which they arise in all beings.
application: This verse is a quiet corrective to the belief that intelligence or capability is something you possess or lack on your own. The clarity you bring to a customer problem, the instinct you feel when spotting a product gap, the energy that carries you through a hard week, none of it originates in you alone. That framing takes pressure off performance and puts attention on being a clear channel rather than proving you have enough to offer. For the move from CSM to PM, the question is less about acquiring credentials and more about letting the discernment you already exercise daily become visible.
question: When you are at your sharpest, what conditions allowed that intelligence to come through, and what usually gets in the way of it?
